vitals.location: Cologne, Germany (Rheinauhafen start/finish, through Bergisches Land)
vitals.start_format: Speed-based rolling start blocks, separate from the professional race start
vitals.entry_fee: Velodom 120: EUR90 (1-500) / EUR105 (501-1,000) / EUR125 thereafter. Velodom 60: EUR70 launch price, rising by tranche.
vitals.cutoff_time: Minimum average speed of 25 km/h including feed stops; riders falling behind can be pulled and recorded DNF
route_options: Velodom 120: 121 km, 1,170 m gain — full Granfondo route sharing much of the professional course
Velodom 60: 65.8 km, 490 m gain — shorter route via the Bensberg cobbles

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 5): vitals corrected from an unsourced 99.8km/1,500m to the organizer-confirmed Velodom 120 course (121km/1,170m) — longer and flatter than the prior figures. Under the repo's dimension rubric this leaves distance=3 supported but conflicts with climbing=4 (1,170m implies lower); the course also includes a cobbled Schloss Bensberg passage that road_surface=1 (fresh tarmac throughout) doesn't account for. Recommend a human re-check climbing and road_surface against these corrected facts. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: Starts and finishes at Rheinauhafen in central Cologne, running out through eastern and northern Cologne into the Bergisches Land before returning. The Velodom 120 pushes further — through the airport area, Lohmar, Overath/Ferrenberg, Kürten and Wipperfeld — while the Velodom 60 takes a shorter loop through Bergisch Gladbach. Both routes share the race's signature obstacle on the way back: a cobbled climb up to Schloss Bensberg, the one non-smooth section on an otherwise fully paved course. The Granfondo waves start separately and earlier than the professional Rund um Köln classic (which begins at 11:30, hours after the Velodom fields), sharing roads and the Rheinauhafen finish line but running as their own timed, ranked races.
course_description.signature_challenge: The cobbled climb to Schloss Bensberg, arriving on both routes after the Bergisches Land rollers have already worn riders down — the defining non-smooth section on an otherwise fully paved course.
suffering_zones: Wand von Sand — the climb out of Cologne into Bergisch Gladbach that opens the real work
Schloss Bensberg cobbles — the race's signature obstacle, hit on both the 60 and 120
The Bergisches Land rollers (Overath, Kürten, Wipperfeld on the 120) — sustained short, sharp climbing rather than one defining ascent
Maintaining the mandatory 25 km/h minimum average, including feed stops, for the full distance — fall behind and you can be pulled and recorded DNF

logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant hotels in Cologne; book early for race weekend.

history.origin_story: The professional Rund um Köln was founded by four Cologne cycling clubs and first held September 13, 1908 — the organizer calls it Germany's oldest surviving road race, with more editions than any other German race. The modern open-entry mass-participation Granfondo/Jedermann product (branded Velodom) is much younger: it premiered in 2004, when non-licensed recreational riders were first allowed onto the professional course. The two histories are related but distinct — don't read the 1908 founding date onto the amateur race itself.
notable_moments: 1908: Professional Rund um Köln founded by four Cologne cycling clubs
2004: Modern open-entry Jedermann/Velodom mass-participation race launched alongside the professional event
2026 (108th edition, May 17): 8,500+ combined Velodom entries; Laurence Pithie won the professional race
2027 (109th edition, May 23): registration opened with Velodom 60 and 120 only — Velodom 30, held in 2026, is not offered
history.reputation: A long-running, well-organized German Jedermann race riding real sections of the country's oldest professional classic, with genuine competitive structure (speed-based start blocks, age categories, team classifications) rather than a loose group ride.

biased_opinion.verdict: Real Competitive Race, Sharing the Old Roads
biased_opinion.summary: The Jedermann-Rennen at Rund um Köln is a genuine, competitively structured mass-participation race, not a curtain-raiser tacked onto a professional event. Riders start in speed-based blocks hours ahead of the professional race, run their own transponder-timed results with age categories and team classifications, and finish under a mandatory 25 km/h minimum average pace. The course shares real roads with Germany's oldest surviving professional race (founded 1908) — including the cobbled climb to Schloss Bensberg — but the modern open-entry Granfondo product itself dates only to 2004; don't confuse the parent race's century-plus pedigree with the amateur event's shorter history. The organizer trimmed its lineup for 2027, dropping the shorter Velodom 30 and offering only the 60 and 120.
strengths: Genuinely separate, competitively structured race — speed blocks, transponder timing, age categories, team classifications — not a loose ride alongside the pros
Real historical roads: shares course sections, including the Schloss Bensberg cobbles, with Germany's oldest surviving professional road race
Strong, growing demand — 2027 registration was more than half sold within a week of opening
Clear competitive standard (25 km/h minimum average) that keeps the field genuinely racing rather than touring
weaknesses: The 1908 founding date belongs to the professional race, not the amateur Jedermann/Velodom product, which only dates to 2004 — treat any '100+ years' framing of the amateur race itself skeptically
The shorter Velodom 30 option was dropped for 2027 — only the 60 and 120 remain
Entry pricing rises in tranches through the registration window (EUR90 to EUR125 on the 120), so late registrants pay meaningfully more
The mandatory minimum average speed means this isn't a casual ride — riders who can't hold 25 km/h risk being pulled from the course
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, well-organized German Jedermann race with real competitive stakes and genuine ties to Rund um Köln's professional heritage — go in ready to hold pace, not to tour.

final_verdict.one_liner: A real competitive Jedermann race on Germany's oldest professional cycling roads — bring race fitness, not tourist legs.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuinely competitive, well-organized German road race with historical roads and clear categories. Skip it if you want a casual group ride — the mandatory 25 km/h minimum average is enforced.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similarly historic German road event with an amateur component, check other Bundesliga-adjacent Jedermann races on the German cycling calendar; for a less competitive touring alternative in the same region, look for a Bergisches Land ADFC-style recreational tour instead.

videos.title: Athletengeschichten: Imster Radmarathon, Rund um Köln, RAN, Schleck Gran Fondo
videos.description:
Hier geht's zum Coaching mit uns: 
https://www.sporformance.de/coaching 

Unser Trainingslager im Ötztal:
https://sporformance.de/oetztal-camp/
________________________________________

Die Saison ist mittlerweile im vollen Gange und unsere Athleten fahren diverse Events! 

Im heutigen Video führt euch Laurin durch spannende Rennen unserer Athleten und zeigt euch einmal die volle Bandbreite. Mit dabei sind Radmarathon's, Jedermann-Rennen und auch ein Ultra-Cycling-Event! 

Ziel ist es, dass ihr mal einen kleinen Einblick bekommt, wie verschiedene Events gefahren werden und was für Leistungsdaten abgerufen werden müssen!

Viel Spaß mit dem Video!

00:00 Einleitung
01:01 Imster Radmarathon
05:08 Rund um Köln (70 km)
7:26 Rund um Köln (120 km) 
10:08 Race around Niederösterreich 
13:08 Schleck Gran Fondo
______________________________________
Folge uns auch hier:
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/sporformance/ 
Facebook:  https://www.facebook.com/sporformance/

videos.title: Rund um Köln: Anton nimmt dich per GoPro mit ins sehr schnelle Rennen!
videos.description:
Unser Coach Anton nimmt dich per GoPro mit in sein hartes Rennen bei Rund um Köln! 

✅  Abonniere unverbindlich diesen Kanal und verpasse keine weiteren nützlichen Radsport-Videos (v.a. Coachingtipps & Analysen) mehr: https://bit.ly/2EBWHwI 

-------------------------------------------------------------
Zum Video 🎥
-------------------------------------------------------------

Unser Coach Anton war bei Rund um Köln am Start und hat dabei die interessantesten Szenen seines Rennens per GoPro gefilmt. 

Im heutigen Video analysiert er diese Szenen und gewährt dir sehr interessante Einblicke :-) 

Alle weiteren Zahlen (Wattwerte), Analysen (inkl. Vergleich Jedermänner vs. Profis!) findest du bei uns auf SpeedVille im Blogpost:

videos.title: Team Casa Ciclista zu Gast bei Moxxa Cafè
videos.description: Casa Ciclista Teamtreffen in Köln: Zum Auftakt der Saison 2019 traf sich ein Großteil des Jedermann-Radteams von Marcel Wüst in der Rheinmetropole zu einen geselligen Abend. Auf dem Programm stand auch ein Empfang in der Rösthalle von Moxxa Cafè. Der Kaffeehersteller ist seit Jahren als Sponsor des Team Casa Ciclista aktiv und mit Dirk Barthelmeß auch selbst auf dem Rad dabei.
